actionUnits.condition: Automatic neighbor-driven ignition. The attached MonoBehaviour component exactly matches the Fire script (m_Script guid=1729fd01c90a64d5b8037e1eef0d000d; component fileID=2592690533154406537) with scene-configured fields: isOn=false, timeToOnMin=40, timeToOnMax=60, onNeighborsThreshold=2, maxNeighborDistance=4, downMultiplier=2, totalHitTime=5, mainEffectGo=fileID 1849464941111748095. Preconditions: neighbors list is populated (via Fire.FindNeighbors or manually) so that onNeighbors >= 2, causing CheckNeighborState() to set shouldTurnOn=true. Then, after Random.Range(40..60)s, Update() flips isOn=true and UpdateFireState(true) plays particles and notifies FireController.Instance.FireChanged(+1). Note: This object has a BoxCollider but no Rigidbody; no UnityEvent m_Calls are present. This Trigger is a timed wait to allow the auto-on path to execute.

Manual extinguish path requires external calls to Fire.WaterHit(Vector3). The Fire script implements IWaterInteractable.WaterHit(Vector3), which sets isTurningOff=true and resets currentHitInterval. To turn the fire off via Update(), WaterHit must be invoked repeatedly so currentHitInterval < maxHitInterval (2s) until currentHitTime >= totalHitTime (5s). There are no public parameterless methods on this component and no UnityEvent m_Calls; therefore, we cannot directly invoke WaterHit with the provided action types. Please supply the water emitter object or an event binding that calls WaterHit on this instance to cover the turn-off branch.
